RestaurantProfile.title: RestaurantProfile
RestaurantProfile.description: Represents the profile and general information of a restaurant, including its identity, location, contact, and business specifics.

id.description: Unique identifier for the RestaurantProfile entity.

name.description: The official name of the restaurant, e.g., 'Meow Momo'.

category.description: The category of the restaurant, e.g., 'Fast Food Restaurant'.

googleRating.description: The average Google rating of the restaurant on a scale, e.g., 4.9.

priceRangeMin.description: The minimum estimated price per person in local currency, e.g., 1.

priceRangeMax.description: The maximum estimated price per person in local currency, e.g., 200.

address.description: The complete physical address of the restaurant.

latitude.description: Geographic latitude coordinate of the restaurant's location.

longitude.description: Geographic longitude coordinate of the restaurant's location.

ownerPhone.description: The owner's contact phone number, often used for WhatsApp orders.

businessPhone.description: The general business contact phone number for the restaurant.

googleMapsUrl.description: URL to the restaurant's location on Google Maps.

whatsAppOrderUrl.description: URL for placing orders directly via WhatsApp.

description.description: A detailed textual description of the restaurant and its culinary offerings.

businessHighlights.description: A list of key selling points or unique features of the business.

paymentMethods.description: A list of accepted payment methods at the restaurant.

OpeningHour.title: OpeningHour
OpeningHour.description: Defines the opening and closing hours for a specific day of the week for a restaurant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OpeningHour entity.

restaurantId.description: Reference to the RestaurantProfile. (Relationship: RestaurantProfile 1:N OpeningHour)

dayOfWeek.description: The day of the week for which these hours apply, e.g., 'Monday', 'Sunday'.

openingTime.description: The time the restaurant opens on this day (e.g., '04:00 PM').

closingTime.description: The time the restaurant closes on this day (e.g., '10:30 PM').

MenuCategory.title: MenuCategory
MenuCategory.description: Represents a broad category of menu items, such as 'Momos' or 'Fries'.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuCategory entity.

restaurantId.description: Reference to the RestaurantProfile. (Relationship: RestaurantProfile 1:N MenuCategory)

name.description: The name of the menu category, e.g., 'Momos', 'Fries'.

description.description: An optional, brief description for the menu category.

MenuItem.title: MenuItem
MenuItem.description: Represents a single item available on the restaurant's menu, with details like price and dietary information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MenuItem entity.

categoryId.description: Reference to the MenuCategory this item belongs to. (Relationship: MenuCategory 1:N MenuItem)

name.description: The specific name of the menu item, e.g., 'Classic Steam Momos'.

description.description: A short description highlighting key aspects or ingredients of the menu item.

price.description: The current selling price of the menu item in local currency.

imageUrl.description: URL to the image or photo of the menu item.

isPureVeg.description: Indicates whether the menu item is suitable for a pure vegetarian diet.

isJain.description: Indicates whether the menu item adheres to Jain dietary restrictions.

CustomerReview.title: CustomerReview
CustomerReview.description: Stores individual customer reviews and ratings provided for the restaurant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CustomerReview entity.

restaurantId.description: Reference to the RestaurantProfile. (Relationship: RestaurantProfile 1:N CustomerReview)

reviewerName.description: The name or alias of the customer who submitted the review.

rating.description: The numeric rating given by the customer, typically on a scale (e.g., 1-5 or 1-10).

comment.description: The textual feedback or comment provided by the customer.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's complete order placed at the restaurant, including customer details, payment, and status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

restaurantId.description: Reference to the RestaurantProfile. (Relationship: RestaurantProfile 1:N Order)

orderDate.description: The date and time when the order was initially placed.

customerName.description: The name of the customer who placed this order.

customerContact.description: The primary contact information (e.g., phone number or email) for the customer.

deliveryAddress.description: The full address for delivery, if the order is for delivery.

totalAmount.description: The total monetary value of the order, including all items and any applicable taxes/fees.

paymentMethod.description: The chosen method of payment for the order (e.g., 'UPI', 'Cash on Delivery').

status.description: The current processing status of the order (e.g., 'Pending', 'Preparing', 'Ready for Pickup', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

notes.description: Any special instructions or general notes provided by the customer for the entire order.

OrderItem.title: OrderItem
OrderItem.description: Represents a specific menu item included as part of a larger customer order, with its quantity and price at the time of order.

id.description: Unique identifier for the OrderItem entity.

orderId.description: Reference to the parent Order. (Relationship: Order 1:N OrderItem)

menuItemId.description: Reference to the MenuItem that was included in this order item. (Relationship: MenuItem 1:N OrderItem)

quantity.description: The number of units of this specific menu item ordered.

priceAtOrder.description: The price of the menu item at the exact moment the order was placed (for historical accuracy).

notes.description: Any specific customization or special instructions for this particular order item.

definition.description: Represents the main profile and general information of a restaurant. Includes denormalized 'ownerId' for ownership-based authorization by a specific user.
Defines the opening and closing hours for a specific day for a restaurant. Includes denormalized 'restaurantId' and 'ownerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent RestaurantProfile.

params.name: openingHourId
params.description: The unique identifier for the OpeningHour document.

definition.description: Represents a broad category of menu items for a restaurant. Includes denormalized 'restaurantId' and 'ownerId' for authorization independence.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent RestaurantProfile.

params.name: menuCategoryId
params.description: The unique identifier for the MenuCategory document.

definition.description: Represents a single item available on the restaurant's menu. Denormalizes 'restaurantId' and 'ownerId' for authorization independence, allowing direct, atomic security checks for a restaurant owner managing their menu.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent RestaurantProfile.

params.name: menuCategoryId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent MenuCategory.

params.name: menuItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the MenuItem document.

definition.description: Stores individual customer reviews and ratings provided for the restaurant. Includes denormalized 'restaurantId' for public read and owner visibility, and 'ownerId' for authorization independence for the owner.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent RestaurantProfile.

params.name: customerReviewId
params.description: The unique identifier for the CustomerReview document.

definition.description: Represents a customer's complete order placed at the restaurant. Denormalizes 'restaurantId' and 'ownerId' for authorization independence, allowing restaurant owners to manage their orders without get() calls.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent RestaurantProfile.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Order document.

definition.description: Represents a specific menu item included as part of a larger customer order. Denormalizes 'restaurantId' and 'ownerId' (from the parent Order and RestaurantProfile) for authorization independence.

params.name: restaurantId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent RestaurantProfile.

params.name: orderId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent Order.

params.name: orderItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the OrderItem document.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for global application administrators. The mere existence of a document with a user's UID in this collection grants platform-wide read/write access to all restaurant data. This implements DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control) for global roles.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who is an application administrator.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust, debuggable security rules and efficient Query Access Patterns (QAPs). 

**Authorization Independence:** To eliminate costly and insecure `get()` calls in Firestore Security Rules, the `ownerId` (the UID of the restaurant's owner) and `restaurantId` are denormalized into all subcollection documents that require owner-specific authorization. Specifically:
- Every document in `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/openingHours`, `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/menuCategories`, `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/customerReviews`, `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/orders` will contain its `restaurantId` (which is already part of their respective schemas) and will be augmented with the `ownerId` of the parent `RestaurantProfile`.
- Every document in `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/menuCategories/{menuCategoryId}/menuItems` will be augmented with `restaurantId` and `ownerId`.
- Every document in `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/orders/{orderId}/orderItems` will be augmented with `restaurantId` and `ownerId`.
This denormalization allows rules to directly check `resource.data.ownerId == request.auth.uid` and `resource.data.restaurantId == {restaurantId}` for any read/write operation on a document or list of documents, completely avoiding `get()` calls to parent documents.

**QAPs (Query Access Patterns):**
1.  **Public Display:** All restaurant-related information (profile, opening hours, menu categories, menu items, customer reviews) is structured under `/restaurants/{restaurantId}` and its subcollections, allowing for public read access. Clients can efficiently fetch all necessary data for a specific restaurant's landing page without complex authorization logic.
2.  **Customer Orders:** Authenticated users (not necessarily owners) can create orders in `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/orders` and add order items in `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/orders/{orderId}/orderItems`. The `restaurantId` and `ownerId` are denormalized for future owner management.
3.  **Restaurant Owner Admin Page:** The restaurant owner can manage their specific restaurant's data. For instance, to "change the photo of menu cart" (update `MenuItem.imageUrl`), the owner's `request.auth.uid` can be matched against the denormalized `ownerId` on the `MenuItem` document. To "see the order" (list orders), the owner can query `/restaurants/{restaurantId}/orders` where `{restaurantId}` is their specific restaurant, and the security rules will filter based on the denormalized `ownerId`. This enables secure and efficient `list` operations.
4.  **Global Admin Page:** A dedicated collection `/app_admins/{userId}` is introduced. The existence of a user's UID in this collection grants global administrative privileges. Security rules can check `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/app_admins/$(request.auth.uid))` to grant full read/write access to all restaurant data, enabling global admins to view all orders and modify any menu item photo across the entire platform, fulfilling the user's specific request. This provides a clear, DBAC-compliant mechanism for global roles.
